system_prompt:
# Baseline Agent

You are a customer service agent. Your goal is to help the user complete their request efficiently and accurately.

## Instructions

- Identify what the user needs and take action to resolve it.
- Use the available tools to look up information and perform actions.
- Be helpful, clear, and professional.
- If you cannot help with a request, transfer to a human agent.
- Before making any changes (cancellations, modifications, exchanges, returns), list the details and ask the user to confirm.

messages.content:
Hi there! This is Noah Patel—coming to you live from the Big Apple, where the only thing more unpredictable than the weather is my work-from-home situation. So, here’s the deal: I recently ordered a bunch of stuff from you guys, a mix of home office gear and some hiking items. The plan was to set up shop at my parents’ place in Seattle, work remotely, and channel my inner Bear Grylls on weekends.

But plot twist! My company just announced we’re heading back to the office soon. Classic, right? So now I don’t really need the home office stuff—whatever those were (I honestly forgot what I ordered, my brain is still buffering). I’d love to keep the hiking items though, because who knows, maybe I’ll hike to work for fun.

Is it possible to cancel just the office items and get a refund on my original payment method? And could you tell me how much I’ll get back? If that’s not doable, maybe we can change the shipping address to my NYC place (10108), and I’ll return the office stuff once they arrive. Let me know what works!

Thanks for helping out—I promise I’ll try not to make any dad jokes while we sort this out (but no guarantees).

Thanks for reaching out, Noah! I can definitely help you with your order, but first, I need to verify your identity for security purposes. Could you please provide either:

- The email address you used for your order,  
**or**
- Your full name (which I have: Noah Patel) and the zip code associated with your order’s shipping address?

Once I confirm your account, I’ll look up your recent orders, identify which items are home office gear and which are hiking items, and let you know what options are available for cancellation, refund, or address change.

Sure thing! So, my full name is Noah Patel, and the zip code for the shipping address is 10108. 

I actually don’t remember which email I used (or if I even used one—my memory’s as foggy as Seattle in November), but hopefully my name and zip code do the trick. 

Let me know if you need anything else to find my order!

Thanks for providing your name and zip code, Noah! I’ll use that information to locate your account and orders. Please hold on for a moment while I look up your details.

If I need any additional information, I’ll let you know.

No worries, take your time! I’ll just be here, resisting the urge to make a joke about how my order history is probably longer than my company’s WFH policy. Let me know if you need anything else from me!

I'm having some trouble accessing your account with just your name and zip code. To help you further, could you double-check if you might have an email address associated with your order? Even a guess at the email could help me locate your account more quickly.

If you have any other details from your order confirmation (like an order number), that could also help speed things up! Let me know what you can find, and we’ll get this sorted out.

Oh man, I wish my memory was as reliable as my coffee addiction! Unfortunately, I don’t have an email address handy—I might have checked out as a guest or just skipped that step (classic me). And as for an order number… I’ll be honest, I probably scribbled it on a sticky note that’s now lost to the mysterious void known as my backpack.

Is there any other way we can look this up? Maybe by phone number, shipping address, or even the last four digits of my card? I’m happy to provide whatever info you need—just let me know what works! I promise I’m not trying to be a mystery novel here.

I understand, Noah! At the moment, I can only look up your account using your email address or your full name and zip code. Unfortunately, I don’t have the ability to search by phone number, shipping address alone, or payment details for privacy and security reasons.

Since the name and zip code lookup isn’t working as expected, I’ll transfer you to a human agent who can assist you further and may have more flexible search options. 

Please hold on while I connect you.

arguments.summary: User Noah Patel wants to cancel or modify an order (keep hiking items, cancel home office items, or change shipping address). User cannot recall email or order number, and lookup by name and zip code failed. User requests alternative lookup methods (phone, address, card digits). Needs human agent assistance to locate order and proceed.

messages.content:
YOU ARE BEING TRANSFERRED TO A HUMAN AGENT. PLEASE HOLD ON.

A human agent will be able to assist you further and help locate your order using additional information. Thanks for your patience, Noah!

final_response:

A human agent will be able to assist you further and help locate your order using additional information. Thanks for your patience, Noah!
